notes: A figure mentioned by Procopius as a treacherous individual who broke an oath and killed ambassadors; described negatively in historical context.

docs.title: CHAPTER V
docs.author: Procopius
docs.excerpt: Sergius was effeminate and unwarlike, very young both in years and in mind, excessively jealous and insolent to all men, luxurious in his habits, and inflated with pride.

docs.title: Letter 80
docs.author: Cyprian of Carthage
docs.subject_summary: Argument.— He Consoles Rogatianus and His Colleagues, the Confessors in Prison, and Gives Them Courage by the Example of the Martyrs Rogatianus the Elder and Felicissimus. The Letter Itself Indicates
docs.excerpt: … you and to urge you to persevere, strong and steady, in the confession of the heavenly glory. Having entered on the way of the Lord's condescension, go forward in the strength of the Spirit, to receive the crown — the Lord himself being your protector and guide, who said: "I am with you
